<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yokose Kofun]][[en:Yokose Kofun]][[ja:横瀬古墳]][[zh:橫瀨古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yokose Kofun]][[en:Yokose Kofun]][[ja:横瀬古墳]][[zh:橫瀨古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yokose Kofun]][[en:Yokose Kofun]][[ja:横瀬古墳]][[zh:橫瀨古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11543317]] Wikidata name: Yokose Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Yokose Kofun | native_name =横瀬古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Yokose Kofun SW 2013.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Yokose Kofun | map_type = Japan Kagoshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yokose Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|31.413361|N|131.014833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Osaki|WD=Q931439}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|24|47.88|N|131|00|53.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yokose Kofun'''|横瀬古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the town of {{ill|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|en|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|WD=Q931439|lt= Ōsaki}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1943. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162718|title=横瀬古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Kagoshima Prefecture after the [[Tōjin Kofun Cluster|Tōjin Ōtsuka Kofun]], and is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late 5th century (middle Kofun period).
==Overview== The Yokose Kofun is located in a {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field}} on the right bank of the Mochidome River, located almost in the center of the Kimotsuki Plain that spreads inside the sand dunes along the coast of Shibushi Bay. It is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun |zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The total length is approximately 135 meters, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 72.5 meters with a height of approximately 13.6 meters. The width at the constriction between the anterior and posterior portions is 45 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a width of 62.5 meters, and height of 9.6 meters, and there is a surrounding moat with a width of 11.3 to 18.7 meters with a depth of 1.5 meters. There are also traces of a surrounding moat on the outside of the northern end of the circular portion and southern end of the rectangular portion, and it is thought that there may have originally been a double ring moat. The tumulus is orientated to the southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It has been known to be an ancient tomb since the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, and there is currently a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} on the anterior portion. A pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the rear circle. It was {{ill|Grave robbery|en|Grave robbery|WD=Q1537376|lt=robbed}} around 1902, and iron swords and other items were unearthed, but [[archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1997 and the following year discovered cylindrical and figurine ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', which are rare in the prefecture, fragments of armor, ''[[magatama]]'', and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery. Two cylindrical ''haniwa'' are on display at the Kagoshima Prefectural Museum. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is approximately 19 minutes by car from {{ill|Shibushi Station|en|Shibushi Station|WD=Q7496088|lt=Shibushi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nichinan Line|en|Nichinan Line|WD=Q871676|lt=Nichinan Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> 横瀬古墳出土 埴輪-1.JPG|''Haniwa'' from the Yokose Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|WD=Q6573269|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yokose Kofun]][[en:Yokose Kofun]][[ja:横瀬古墳]][[zh:橫瀨古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yokose Kofun]][[en:Yokose Kofun]][[ja:横瀬古墳]][[zh:橫瀨古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yokose Kofun]][[en:Yokose Kofun]][[ja:横瀬古墳]][[zh:橫瀨古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11543317]] Wikidata name: Yokose Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Yokose Kofun | native_name =横瀬古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Yokose Kofun SW 2013.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Yokose Kofun | map_type = Japan Kagoshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yokose Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|31.413361|N|131.014833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Osaki|WD=Q931439}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|24|47.88|N|131|00|53.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yokose Kofun'''|横瀬古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the town of {{ill|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|en|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|WD=Q931439|lt= Ōsaki}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1943. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162718|title=横瀬古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Kagoshima Prefecture after the [[Tōjin Kofun Cluster|Tōjin Ōtsuka Kofun]], and is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late 5th century (middle Kofun period).
==Overview== The Yokose Kofun is located in a {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field}} on the right bank of the Mochidome River, located almost in the center of the Kimotsuki Plain that spreads inside the sand dunes along the coast of Shibushi Bay. It is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun |zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The total length is approximately 135 meters, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 72.5 meters with a height of approximately 13.6 meters. The width at the constriction between the anterior and posterior portions is 45 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a width of 62.5 meters, and height of 9.6 meters, and there is a surrounding moat with a width of 11.3 to 18.7 meters with a depth of 1.5 meters. There are also traces of a surrounding moat on the outside of the northern end of the circular portion and southern end of the rectangular portion, and it is thought that there may have originally been a double ring moat. The tumulus is orientated to the southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It has been known to be an ancient tomb since the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, and there is currently a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} on the anterior portion. A pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the rear circle. It was {{ill|Grave robbery|en|Grave robbery|WD=Q1537376|lt=robbed}} around 1902, and iron swords and other items were unearthed, but [[archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1997 and the following year discovered cylindrical and figurine ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', which are rare in the prefecture, fragments of armor, ''[[magatama]]'', and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery. Two cylindrical ''haniwa'' are on display at the Kagoshima Prefectural Museum. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is approximately 19 minutes by car from {{ill|Shibushi Station|en|Shibushi Station|WD=Q7496088|lt=Shibushi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nichinan Line|en|Nichinan Line|WD=Q871676|lt=Nichinan Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> 横瀬古墳出土 埴輪-1.JPG|''Haniwa'' from the Yokose Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|WD=Q6573269|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)}}

It is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun |zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The total length is approximately 135 meters, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 72.5 meters with a height of approximately 13.6 meters. The width at the constriction between the anterior and posterior portions is 45 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a width of 62.5 meters, and height of 9.6 meters, and there is a surrounding moat with a width of 11.3 to 18.7 meters with a depth of 1.5 meters. There are also traces of a surrounding moat on the outside of the northern end of the circular portion and southern end of the rectangular portion, and it is thought that there may have originally been a double ring moat. The tumulus is orientated to the southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It has been known to be an ancient tomb since the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, and there is currently a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} on the anterior portion. A pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the rear circle. It was {{ill|Grave robbery|en|Grave robbery|WD=Q1537376|lt=robbed}} around 1902, and iron swords and other items were unearthed, but [[archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1997 and the following year discovered cylindrical and figurine ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', which are rare in the prefecture, fragments of armor, ''[[magatama]]'', and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery. Two cylindrical ''haniwa'' are on display at the Kagoshima Prefectural Museum. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is approximately 19 minutes by car from {{ill|Shibushi Station|en|Shibushi Station|WD=Q7496088|lt=Shibushi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nichinan Line|en|Nichinan Line|WD=Q871676|lt=Nichinan Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yokose Kofun]][[en:Yokose Kofun]][[ja:横瀬古墳]][[zh:橫瀨古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yokose Kofun]][[en:Yokose Kofun]][[ja:横瀬古墳]][[zh:橫瀨古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Yokose Kofun]][[en:Yokose Kofun]][[ja:横瀬古墳]][[zh:橫瀨古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Yokose Kofun | native_name =横瀬古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Yokose Kofun SW 2013.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Yokose Kofun | map_type = Japan Kagoshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yokose Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|31.413361|N|131.014833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Osaki|WD=Q931439|1=Ōsaki, Kagoshima}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|24|47.88|N|131|00|53.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yokose Kofun'''|横瀬古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the town of {{ill|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|en|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|WD=Q931439|lt= Ōsaki|1=Ōsaki, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1943. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162718|title=横瀬古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Kagoshima Prefecture after the [[Tōjin Kofun Cluster|Tōjin Ōtsuka Kofun]], and is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late 5th century (middle Kofun period).
==Overview== The Yokose Kofun is located in a {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field|1=Paddy field}} on the right bank of the Mochidome River, located almost in the center of the Kimotsuki Plain that spreads inside the sand dunes along the coast of Shibushi Bay. It is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun |zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The total length is approximately 135 meters, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 72.5 meters with a height of approximately 13.6 meters. The width at the constriction between the anterior and posterior portions is 45 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a width of 62.5 meters, and height of 9.6 meters, and there is a surrounding moat with a width of 11.3 to 18.7 meters with a depth of 1.5 meters. There are also traces of a surrounding moat on the outside of the northern end of the circular portion and southern end of the rectangular portion, and it is thought that there may have originally been a double ring moat. The tumulus is orientated to the southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It has been known to be an ancient tomb since the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, and there is currently a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} on the anterior portion. A pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the rear circle. It was {{ill|Grave robbery|en|Grave robbery|WD=Q1537376|lt=robbed|1=Grave robbery}} around 1902, and iron swords and other items were unearthed, but [[archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1997 and the following year discovered cylindrical and figurine ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', which are rare in the prefecture, fragments of armor, ''[[magatama]]'', and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery. Two cylindrical ''haniwa'' are on display at the Kagoshima Prefectural Museum. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is approximately 19 minutes by car from {{ill|Shibushi Station|en|Shibushi Station|WD=Q7496088|lt=Shibushi Station|1=Shibushi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nichinan Line|en|Nichinan Line|WD=Q871676|lt=Nichinan Line|1=Nichinan Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> 横瀬古墳出土 埴輪-1.JPG|''Haniwa'' from the Yokose Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|WD=Q6573269|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpōkōenfun]] | name = Yokose Kofun | native_name =横瀬古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Yokose Kofun SW 2013.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Yokose Kofun | map_type = Japan Kagoshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yokose Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|31.413361|N|131.014833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Osaki|WD=Q931439|1=Ōsaki, Kagoshima}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|24|47.88|N|131|00|53.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yokose Kofun'''|横瀬古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the town of {{ill|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|en|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|WD=Q931439|lt= Ōsaki|1=Ōsaki, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1943. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162718|title=横瀬古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Kagoshima Prefecture after the [[Tōjin Kofun Cluster|Tōjin Ōtsuka Kofun]], and is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late 5th century (middle Kofun period).
==Overview== The Yokose Kofun is located in a {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field|1=Paddy field}} on the right bank of the Mochidome River, located almost in the center of the Kimotsuki Plain that spreads inside the sand dunes along the coast of Shibushi Bay. It is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpōkōenfun |zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|1=[[Zenpōkōenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The total length is approximately 135 meters, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 72.5 meters with a height of approximately 13.6 meters. The width at the constriction between the anterior and posterior portions is 45 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a width of 62.5 meters, and height of 9.6 meters, and there is a surrounding moat with a width of 11.3 to 18.7 meters with a depth of 1.5 meters. There are also traces of a surrounding moat on the outside of the northern end of the circular portion and southern end of the rectangular portion, and it is thought that there may have originally been a double ring moat. The tumulus is orientated to the southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It has been known to be an ancient tomb since the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, and there is currently a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} on the anterior portion. A pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the rear circle. It was {{ill|Grave robbery|en|Grave robbery|WD=Q1537376|lt=robbed|1=Grave robbery}} around 1902, and iron swords and other items were unearthed, but [[archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1997 and the following year discovered cylindrical and figurine ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', which are rare in the prefecture, fragments of armor, ''[[magatama]]'', and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery. Two cylindrical ''haniwa'' are on display at the Kagoshima Prefectural Museum. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is approximately 19 minutes by car from {{ill|Shibushi Station|en|Shibushi Station|WD=Q7496088|lt=Shibushi Station|1=Shibushi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nichinan Line|en|Nichinan Line|WD=Q871676|lt=Nichinan Line|1=Nichinan Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> 横瀬古墳出土 埴輪-1.JPG|''Haniwa'' from the Yokose Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|WD=Q6573269|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] | name = Yokose Kofun | native_name =横瀬古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Yokose Kofun SW 2013.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Yokose Kofun | map_type = Japan Kagoshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yokose Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|31.413361|N|131.014833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Osaki|WD=Q931439|1=Ōsaki, Kagoshima}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|24|47.88|N|131|00|53.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yokose Kofun'''|横瀬古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the town of {{ill|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|en|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|WD=Q931439|lt= Ōsaki|1=Ōsaki, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1943. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162718|title=横瀬古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Kagoshima Prefecture after the [[Tōjin Kofun Cluster|Tōjin Ōtsuka Kofun]], and is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late 5th century (middle Kofun period).
==Overview== The Yokose Kofun is located in a {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field|1=Paddy field}} on the right bank of the Mochidome River, located almost in the center of the Kimotsuki Plain that spreads inside the sand dunes along the coast of Shibushi Bay. It is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpō-kōen-fun |zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The total length is approximately 135 meters, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 72.5 meters with a height of approximately 13.6 meters. The width at the constriction between the anterior and posterior portions is 45 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a width of 62.5 meters, and height of 9.6 meters, and there is a surrounding moat with a width of 11.3 to 18.7 meters with a depth of 1.5 meters. There are also traces of a surrounding moat on the outside of the northern end of the circular portion and southern end of the rectangular portion, and it is thought that there may have originally been a double ring moat. The tumulus is orientated to the southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It has been known to be an ancient tomb since the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, and there is currently a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} on the anterior portion. A pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the rear circle. It was {{ill|Grave robbery|en|Grave robbery|WD=Q1537376|lt=robbed|1=Grave robbery}} around 1902, and iron swords and other items were unearthed, but [[archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1997 and the following year discovered cylindrical and figurine ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', which are rare in the prefecture, fragments of armor, ''[[magatama]]'', and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery. Two cylindrical ''haniwa'' are on display at the Kagoshima Prefectural Museum. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is approximately 19 minutes by car from {{ill|Shibushi Station|en|Shibushi Station|WD=Q7496088|lt=Shibushi Station|1=Shibushi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nichinan Line|en|Nichinan Line|WD=Q871676|lt=Nichinan Line|1=Nichinan Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
